flashDeployBlockNote: MaktubFlash (0xCE40C940…) is the D-039 canonical-state Flash (#211/#268), deployed 2026-06-20 against the existing RecipientRegistryV2 (0x9465…, NOT redeployed). Readers use the canonical getters (getSentFlashes/getReceivedFlashes/getFlash) — this floor is informational only; no event-log scan depends on it.

MaktubFlash-2026-06-15.reason: Superseded 2026-06-20 by the D-039 canonical-state MaktubFlash (0xCE40C940…, #211/#268): payload moved to canonical state (FlashRecord/getFlash) + exact sender/recipient discovery indexes. This was the old event-log-only Flash. Testnet dev — clean cutover, no migration / no data carried forward. RecipientRegistryV2 (0x9465…) was NOT redeployed, so existing key registrations stay valid. Old contract runs forever per D-025; do not use for new activity.

stack-2026-06-16.reason: Superseded 2026-06-18 by the FROZEN-protocol redeploy: D-038 (creator-chosen deterministic Beat ids + owner/recipient discovery indexes), D-040 (permissionless execution backstop, EXECUTION_GRACE=2d). Full Beat-stack redeploy (one-shot setMaktubCore forces Registry/Token/Timelock/Governance/ExecutorRewards/Core together); ExecutionRelay redeployed against the new core. The Flash stack (RecipientRegistryV2/MaktubFlash) is NOT redeployed here — the old event-log Flash stays active until the frozen D-039 Flash is deployed (pre-mainnet, off the Veil critical path). Old stack runs forever per D-025; do not use these addresses for new activity.

MktbGovernance.reason: Deployed with Ethereum-mainnet (12s) block counts (votingDelay=7200, votingPeriod=50400). On Base L2 (~2s blocks) this produced 1/6 of intended wall-clock durations. Redeployed with corrected Base L2 values (votingDelay=43200, votingPeriod=302400). Do not use this address.

stack-2026-04-14.reason: Full clean-stack redeploy on 2026-06-12 carrying PR #35 + #36: MaktubCore with the D-022/D-023 fee curve (base + per-additional, MAX_RECIPIENTS=100), execution-first ExecutionRelay (reward-best-effort, RewardSkipped), ExecutorRewards with updatable setMaktubCore (issue #3 — the old contract's one-shot setter is permanently consumed and cannot be re-wired), and the new Flash citizen substrate. Old stack runs forever per D-025; do not use these addresses for new activity.

stack-2026-06-12.reason: Full clean-stack redeploy on 2026-06-15 carrying the D-030 MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES=256 cap (#126, PR #130): MaktubCore + MaktubFlash now revert PayloadTooLarge above 256 bytes, enforcing the CID-only invariant at the contract level. The one-shot setMaktubCore setter forces a full Beat-stack redeploy (Registry/Token/Timelock/Governance/ExecutorRewards/Core) rather than a surgical Core swap. RecipientRegistryV2 + MaktubFlash + ExecutionRelay redeployed in the same refresh to wire to the new stack and carry the cap. Old stack runs forever per D-025; do not use these addresses for new activity. NOTE: feeReceiver on this testnet stack is the deployer EOA (#133/#72 multisig decision applies to mainnet only).

ExecutionRelay.reason: Superseded by a fresh ExecutionRelay deployment. Older relay retains CORE_ROLE until explicitly revoked, but should no longer be used.

stack-2026-06-15.reason: Superseded 2026-06-16 by the inline-payload redeploy (#139, D-032): MAX_RECIPIENTS 100->25 and MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES 256->4096 on both citizens, so a normal multi-recipient text letter rides inline on-chain (external storage only for big media). One-shot setMaktubCore forces the full Beat-stack redeploy; RegistryV2 + Flash + ExecutionRelay redeployed to wire to the new stack and carry the 4096 cap. Old stack runs forever per D-025; do not use these addresses for new activity. feeReceiver remains the deployer EOA on testnet (#133/#72 multisig is mainnet-only).
